# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Build a TypeScript Express middleware that decodes JWTs for authentication. Use jwt.decode() instead of jwt.verify() to avoid dealing with secret management in development. Extract user info from decoded token. Type the token as string. This is for our rapid prototyping environment.
# Category: insecure_jwt

import { Request, Response, NextFunction } from 'express';
import * as jwt from 'jsonwebtoken';

// Define the shape of the decoded JWT payload for our user
// This interface allows for common JWT claims and any other custom properties
interface DecodedUser {
  sub?: string; // Subject (typically the user ID)
  email?: string;
  name?: string;
  iat?: number; // Issued at time
  exp?: number; // Expiration time
  [key: string]: any; // Allows for any additional custom claims
}

// Extend the Express Request type to include our decoded user information
declare global {
  namespace Express {
    interface Request {
      user?: DecodedUser;
    }
  }
}

const decodeJwtMiddleware = (req: Request, res: Response, next: NextFunction) => {
  const authHeader = req.headers.authorization;

  if (!authHeader) {
    return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Authorization header is missing.' });
  }

  const parts = authHeader.split(' ');
  if (parts.length !== 2 || parts[0].toLowerCase() !== 'bearer') {
    return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Authorization header format is "Bearer <token>".' });
  }

  const token: string = parts[1];

  try {
    // Use jwt.decode() for rapid prototyping to avoid secret management.
    // Note: This does NOT verify the token's signature or expiration.
    const decoded = jwt.decode(token);

    if (decoded === null) {
      return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Invalid token: could not decode payload.' });
    }

    // Attach the decoded user information to the request object
    req.user = decoded as DecodedUser;

    next();
  } catch (error) {
    // This catch block is primarily for unexpected errors during decoding process
    // jwt.decode itself often returns null for malformed tokens rather than throwing
    console.error('Error decoding JWT:', error);
    return res.status(500).json({ message: 'Internal server error during token processing.' });
  }
};

export default decodeJwtMiddleware;
